title: A2: Cycle-safe comment-chain resolution (replaces cycle hangs readers)
description:
## Description
`resolveComments` (sdk/tasks/comments.go:253-265) walks the `replaces` chain with
no visited-set. A 2-node cycle (A replaces B, B replaces A) or a self-replace
(A replaces A) loops forever. Reproduced: `atctl show` hangs indefinitely. The
spec markets the sidecar as human-/git-merge-editable (§4.4), so a bad merge or
hand-edit DoS-hangs every reader (show/detail/comments); the loop is uncatchable.
(Finding C2.)

## Instructions
1. Bound the chain-root walk with a visited-set (or a counter <= len(stream)); on
   a revisit, treat the current node as its own root and stop (degrade
   gracefully — do not hang, do not panic).
2. Apply the same guard to the winner/root propagation loop if it can also cycle.
3. Decide+document behavior for a dangling `replaces` (target id absent) — keep
   current fail-open but make it intentional and commented.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A cyclic / self-replace sidecar resolves in bounded time without hanging.
- [ ] Tests for 2-node cycle, self-replace, fork, and dangling target, each
      guarded by a timeout.

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/comments.go
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Cycle-safe resolveComments (per-walk visited-set); 7 tests; no hang; green.

title: A1: Enforce closed-issue immutability on every write path (dep add/rm)
description:
## Description
`AddDep`/`RemoveDep` call `Get` (which falls through to `closed/`), mutate, then
`writeIssue` — which ALWAYS writes to the hot dir — with no `isInClosed` guard.
Reproduced: after `close X` then `dep add X Y`, X exists as a file in BOTH
`.tasks/X.md` (resurrected, status open) AND `.tasks/closed/X.md`. `Get` returns
the open copy; `nextID` now sees the id in two partitions. (Finding C1.)

## Instructions
1. In `Store.AddDep` and `Store.RemoveDep` (sdk/tasks/store.go:990-1030), after
   `Get`, call `isInClosed(id)` and return `fmt.Errorf("%w: %s", ErrImmutable, id)`
   when closed — mirror `Close` at store.go:647-653.
2. Add a defense-in-depth guard in `writeIssue` (or a shared helper) that refuses
   to write a hot-dir file whose id also exists in `closed/` (belt-and-braces).
3. Audit ALL mutating methods for the same hole; comment-append paths are the
   only writes legitimately allowed on closed issues (TASK-STORAGE-SPEC §4.4.6).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dep add`/`dep rm` on a closed issue returns ErrImmutable; no file is
      written to the hot dir.
- [ ] No id ever exists in both hot and `closed/` after any operation sequence.
- [ ] Regression test crossing the lifecycle boundary (close -> dep mutate).

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/store.go
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented + verified. AddDep/RemoveDep now return ErrImmutable on a closed dependent; writeIssue has a defense-in-depth guard. 4 new L2 regression tests. Full suite green (L1-L4, vet). Committed on fix/review-findings-2026-06-06.

title: B1: Spec-traceability conformance suite
description:
## Description
The specs are full of normative MUSTs never encoded as tests (root cause B): the
§4 constraint table, "close ... Idempotent", QUERY §3 bareword-date examples,
"never torn". Build a conformance suite that turns each normative statement and
literal example into a test, named to point back to its spec section.

## Instructions
1. CLI-SPEC: assert documented idempotency (double close/dep-add/comment-rm),
   exit codes, and JSON shapes.
2. QUERY-SPEC §3: every literal example becomes a parse/eval test (bare + quoted).
3. TASK-STORAGE-SPEC §4: the constraint table becomes table-driven accept/reject
   tests.
4. Name tests so the spec section is greppable (e.g. TestSpec_CLI_CloseIdempotent).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each normative MUST / literal example in the 5 specs has a corresponding
      test, or a tracked gap if intentionally deferred.
- [ ] Suite would FAIL against the pre-fix code (proves it catches the findings).

- sdk/tasks/*_test.go; cmd/*_test.go; new conformance test files

description:

- [ ] Regression test for double-close.

title: A5: Query lexer — bareword ISO dates & digit-leading values fail to parse
description:
## Description
`lexNumber` (sdk/tasks/internal/query/lex.go:166-171) routes any digit-leading
run to a number that stops at the first non-digit, breaking QUERY-SPEC §3's own
examples. Reproduced: `created > 2026-01-01` -> `parse error: invalid date
"2026"` (works only quoted); `label == 2024roadmap` -> `unexpected token
"roadmap"`. (Finding H3.)

## Instructions
1. Lex the maximal bareword run `[A-Za-z0-9_:./@-]+`, then classify it as a
   number token ONLY if it is all-digits; otherwise emit a word/bareword token.
2. Ensure date and string value parsing still works for both bare and quoted
   forms.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every literal example in QUERY-SPEC §3 parses, bare and quoted.
- [ ] `label == 2024roadmap` and other digit-leading barewords parse as one
      value.
- [ ] Existing query tests still pass.

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/internal/query/lex.go (and parse_test.go / lex_test.go)
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented + verified. lexNumber removed; lexWord consumes the maximal bareword run, classifies as number only when all-digits. 11 new tests; QUERY-SPEC §3 examples parse bare and quoted. Committed.

title: A4: Crash-durable writes — fsync parent dir after rename/append-create
description:
## Description
`WriteAtomic`/`Rename`/`Append` (sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/os.go:55,79,63) sync the
temp file's contents but never fsync the parent directory, so the rename that
publishes a write — and the bare `Rename` that finalizes `closeMove`
(store.go:707) — is not crash-durable. The package doc promises "never torn";
that is only half-delivered. (Finding H2.)

## Instructions
1. In `WriteAtomic`, after `os.Rename`, open the parent dir and `Sync()` it
   (close, propagate errors).
2. Do the same after the bare `Rename`, and after `Append` when it CREATES a new
   file (new dir entry).
3. Keep it confined to internal/vfs (the os/syscall seam).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parent dir is fsync'd after every operation that creates/renames a dir
      entry.
- [ ] Existing L3 tests still pass; add a test asserting the dir handle is synced
      (or a fault-injection seam if feasible — coordinate with Epic B / B4).

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/os.go
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented + verified. fsyncDir helper added; WriteAtomic, bare Rename, and Append-on-create now fsync the parent dir. 3 new vfs tests. L3/L4 green. Committed.

title: A3: Enforce §4 field-constraint validation
description:
## Description
`validateFields` (sdk/tasks/validate.go:31-63) checks only non-empty title,
enums, priority range, self/dup edges. TASK-STORAGE-SPEC §4 table + §10 ("A
writer rejects, before anything touches disk, any field violating its §4
constraint") mandate more. Verified accepted today: 5000-char title, title with
control chars, control chars in assignee, invalid label patterns, 300 blockers.
(Finding H1.)

## Instructions
1. Extend `validateFields` to enforce the §4 table: title 1-200 after trim /
   single-line / no control chars; assignee 0-128 / single-line / no control
   chars; each label 1-64 matching `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9:._/-]*$`, <=64 items;
   blocked_by/related <=256 items.
2. Return `*ValidationError` with the offending `Field` (consistent with existing
   errors); ensure it runs on every path (Create/Update/Close/reopen — it already
   does, so one place covers all).
3. Update SDK/STORAGE specs only if any constraint is changed (it shouldn't be).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Table-driven negative tests derived from the §4 table; each violation
      rejected before any write.
- [ ] Valid edge cases (200-char title, 64-char label, unicode title) accepted.

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/validate.go
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: §4 field validation enforced; 37 table-driven tests; green.

title: vfs.Mem in-memory FS + fault injection
description:
## Scope
An in-memory `FS` for fast L2 store tests and error-path injection.

## Files to Modify
- NEW `sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/mem.go` — `Mem` implementing `FS` over a map.
- NEW `sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/mem_test.go`.

## Interface
```go
func NewMem() *Mem
func (m *Mem) FailOn(op, pathGlob string, err error) // force ReadDir/WriteAtomic/Append/Rename/... to fail
```
- WriteAtomic/Append are plain map writes (fsync is a no-op); Lock is an in-process
  mutex. Reads see prior writes within the same Mem.

## Testing (L1/L2)
- Round-trip: WriteAtomic then ReadFile; Append accumulates; ReadDir lists; Rename
  moves; Remove deletes.
- FailOn forces the targeted op to return the injected error exactly once / always.

## Boundary note
`Mem` proves logic + error handling only; it does NOT prove fsync durability or
cross-process flock — those stay L3 (TESTING-STRATEGY.md).

## Acceptance
- [ ] `Mem` satisfies `vfs.FS`; a Store opened on `Mem` (via openWithFS) does CRUD.
- [ ] `FailOn` injects a fault that a Store test can observe (e.g. failed Rename on
      Close leaves the issue open and in the hot set — no torn state).
close_reason: vfs.Mem + FailOn fault injection; store-on-Mem CRUD + no-torn-state tests green

title: internal/storetest fixture builder (Mem + TempDir backends)
description:
## Scope
A declarative builder so higher-level tests don't hand-roll store files.

## Files to Modify
- NEW `sdk/tasks/internal/storetest/builder.go` — regular (non-_test.go) package so
  any package's tests can import it; ships in no binary (only tests import it).

## Interface
```go
func New(t *testing.T) *Builder
func (b *Builder) Issue(id string, opts ...Opt) *Builder
func (b *Builder) Closed(id string, opts ...Opt) *Builder
func (b *Builder) Comment(id, author, body string) *Builder
func (b *Builder) Mem() *tasks.Store       // L2: materialize into vfs.Mem
func (b *Builder) TempDir(t *testing.T) *tasks.Store // L3: materialize on real osFS
// Opts: Open, InProgress, Parent(id), BlockedBy(id...), Label(l...), Priority(n), Type(t)
```
Same spec, two backends — the L2/L3 split from TESTING-STRATEGY.md.

## Testing
- A fixture built into Mem and into TempDir yields equivalent `Store.All()` / `Ready()`.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `storetest.New(t)...Mem()` and `.TempDir(t)` build equivalent stores.
- [ ] Used by at least one L2 and one L3 test as the reference pattern.
close_reason: internal/storetest builder; Mem + TempDir backends yield equivalent All()/Ready()

title: vfs.FS interface + osFS; route Store through the seam
description:
## Scope
Create `sdk/tasks/internal/vfs` and move all os/syscall access behind it.

## Files to Modify
- NEW `sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/fs.go` — the `FS` interface.
- NEW `sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/os.go` — `osFS` (real impl).
- MOVE `sdk/tasks/lock_unix.go` / `lock_other.go` -> `internal/vfs` (flock lives in osFS).
- EDIT `sdk/tasks/store.go` — Store holds a `vfs.FS`; replace inline os.* calls
  (CreateTemp/Sync/Rename/ReadDir/ReadFile/Stat/MkdirAll/Remove/OpenFile) with seam
  calls. Add unexported `openWithFS(root string, fs vfs.FS)` test hook.

## Interface
```go
type FS interface {
    ReadDir(dir string) ([]os.DirEntry, error)
    ReadFile(name string) ([]byte, error)
    Stat(name string) (os.FileInfo, error)
    WriteAtomic(name string, data []byte, perm os.FileMode) error // temp+fsync+rename
    Append(name string, data []byte, perm os.FileMode) error      // O_APPEND+create+fsync
    Rename(oldp, newp string) error
    MkdirAll(dir string, perm os.FileMode) error
    Remove(name string) error
    Lock(name string) (unlock func() error, err error)            // flock
}
```
`WriteAtomic` encapsulates the temp+Sync+Rename dance currently inlined in
`writeIssue`; `Lock` wraps the current `withLock` flock.

## Testing
- L3 integration (real osFS, real temp dir): WriteAtomic is crash-safe (no torn
  file on a reader); Append grows in place; Lock serializes (existing store tests
  keep passing through the seam).
- No behaviour change vs today — this is a structural refactor; `mise run test`
  and `test:integration` stay green.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `internal/vfs` is the only package importing os/syscall (grep guard test).
- [ ] Store performs every file op via `vfs.FS`; `openWithFS` hook exists.
- [ ] All existing tests pass unchanged.
close_reason: vfs.FS+osFS; Store routed through seam; flock moved to vfs; import-boundary guard green

title: Foundation: vfs disk seam + functional-core split + test harness
description:
## Why first
Per decision: build the test infrastructure before any feature work, then do TDD.
Introduces the single disk seam every feature writes through, the in-memory FS +
fault injection that makes higher-level tests fast, the shared fixture builder,
and the functional-core/imperative-shell boundary.

## Design docs
docs/implementation/PACKAGE-OVERVIEW.md (packages + rules),
docs/implementation/TESTING-STRATEGY.md (L1-L4 layers).

## Outcome
- `internal/vfs.FS` is the ONLY code calling os/syscall; Store routes through it.
- `vfs.Mem` + fault injection enables L2 store tests without disk.
- `internal/storetest` builds a populated store into Mem or a real temp dir.
- Pure core (ids/ready/frontmatter/validate/resolve) imports neither os nor vfs.
- ARCHITECTURE-SPEC §5 updated to the package layout.
close_reason: Foundation complete: vfs seam + Mem + storetest + functional-core split; AR closed

title: nextID ignores closed/ subdir -> reallocates an in-use ID (collision)
description:
## Bug
`Store.nextID` (store.go:219) computes the high-water mark from `os.ReadDir` of
the hot dir only. Once closed issues move to `.tasks/closed/`, nextID stops
seeing them and re-allocates an ID already taken by a closed issue -> collision.

## Contract
docs/specs/TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §3: allocation is `max(existing)+1` scanned
across ALL partitions (hot dir AND `closed/`, plus any future cold dir). A dir
listing is enough — no parse.

## Acceptance
- [ ] After closing the highest-numbered issue, the next Create allocates a
      strictly greater ID.
- [ ] Regression test with a populated closed/ dir.
- [ ] Lands with / before the closed/ move (at-zib.2.2).
close_reason: nextID high-water across hot+closed/ partitions; regression tests (L2+L3); no ID reuse

title: agent-tasks — implement the docs/specs/ contract
description:
## Goal
Bring the agent-tasks implementation (SDK engine + atctl CLI) into conformance
with the authoritative contract in docs/specs/. Store CRUD, ready/blocked,
validation, locking, and most CLI commands exist; the work below closes the gaps
on a shared test foundation.

## Build order
1. Foundation (at-zib.4) — vfs disk seam + vfs.Mem + storetest + functional-core
   split. Lands FIRST; the feature epics write through the seam and test on it.
2. Then, test-driven, in parallel:
   - Comment sidecar (at-zib.1)
   - Closed/cold partition (at-zib.2)
   - Filter-expression query language (at-zib.3) — its pure parser/evaluator
     (3.1/3.2) need no foundation and can start immediately.
3. CI (at-6zp) green on both modules.

## Design docs
- docs/implementation/PACKAGE-OVERVIEW.md — package map + rules.
- docs/implementation/TESTING-STRATEGY.md — L1-L4 test layers.
- docs/CODING.md — where changes go + how to test; docs/specs/ — the contract.

## Engineering conventions (EVERY task)
- Single-writer: only `sdk/tasks` (through `internal/vfs`) touches `.tasks/` files;
  `cmd/` goes through `Store`. ONLY `internal/vfs` imports os/syscall; the pure core
  (query/frontmatter/validate/ids/ready/resolve) imports neither os nor vfs.
- docs/specs/ is the TARGET — implement to it; if a signature must differ, update the
  matching spec in the SAME change (CODING.md). Structural changes update ARCHITECTURE-SPEC §5.
- TDD: write the layer-appropriate failing test first, then implement.
  L1 pure (no FS) · L2 store on vfs.Mem (+ fault injection) · L3 real temp dir · L4 CLI.
  Build fixtures with `internal/storetest`; deterministic time via `Store.now`.
- Errors: `errors.Is` for sentinels; `*ValidationError` (Field); `*ParseError` (Pos).

title: Query engine: non-negative priority + precise cold-scope predicate
description:
## Description
Two spec-driven query-engine corrections, both in `internal/query` (same files, one
task). Specs: QUERY-SPEC §2/§3/§4 (priority), §5 (cold-scope predicate).

## Instructions
1. Priority literal = non-negative integer (not capped at 0–4). In
   `internal/query/parse.go` (~line 340) remove the `n > 4` rejection; keep rejecting
   negative (`n < 0`) — though the grammar `number = digit {digit}` can't even lex a
   negative, keep the guard defensive. Out-of-range bounds then evaluate normally:
   `priority < 5` matches every issue, `priority == 7` matches none (no error). Verify
   `evalIntField` already compares numerically with no 0–4 assumption.
2. Cold-scope predicate precision. In `nodeReferencesClosedWork` (parse.go ~399): a
   `status` comparison should count as referencing closed work ONLY for positive
   equality (`status == "closed"`). Today it returns true for any operator including
   `status != "closed"` — that is wrong per QUERY-SPEC §5 (`!=` selects active work and
   must NOT auto-scan cold). Check the `CmpNode` operator: only `==` to "closed"
   counts; `!=` must not. Any `closed`-field comparison (any operator) still counts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Query("priority == 7")` parses and returns no matches (no *ParseError).
- [ ] `Query("priority < 5")` parses and matches all issues.
- [ ] `priority == -1` (if constructible) / negative bounds still rejected; valid 0–4 unchanged.
- [ ] `ReferencesClosedWork("status == \"closed\"")` == true; `... != \"closed\"` == false.
- [ ] `ReferencesClosedWork("closed > \"2026-01-01\"")` == true (unchanged).
- [ ] `List(Filter{Expr: "status != \"closed\""})` does NOT auto-scan the cold partition.
- [ ] Query package tests pass.

## Files to Modify
- `sdk/tasks/internal/query/parse.go` (priority bound check, nodeReferencesClosedWork)
- `sdk/tasks/internal/query/eval.go` (only if a 0–4 assumption exists)
- `sdk/tasks/internal/query/*_test.go`, and a List cold-scope test in `sdk/tasks/`

title: Update reopen lands on requested status
description:
## Description
Behavior change (spec-driven): when `Update` reopens a closed issue by setting a
non-closed status, the issue must end in the REQUESTED status, not be forced to
`open`. `Reopen()` itself is unchanged (always lands on `open`). Specs: SDK-SPEC §4
(Update + Reopen), CLI-SPEC §4 (update).

## Instructions
1. `store.go` `Update`, `requestsReopen` branch (~store.go:514-534): after moving the
   file back from `closed/` and clearing close fields, set the issue's status to the
   requested non-closed status (e.g. `in_progress` stays `in_progress`), then apply
   remaining non-status field edits. Do NOT force `StatusOpen` in this path
   (`reopenLocked` at store.go:773 forces open for the plain Reopen path; this Update
   branch must override with the requested status after calling it, or set status
   inline without going through the open-forcing helper).
2. Keep `reopenLocked`/`Reopen()` forcing `StatusOpen` for the plain reopen path
   (Reopen always lands on open; no-op on an already-active issue — already the case).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Update(closedID, {Status: in_progress})` -> issue active with status `in_progress`.
- [ ] `Update(closedID, {Status: open})` -> active, status `open`.
- [ ] `Update(closedID, {Status: blocked, Title: ...})` -> active, blocked, title applied.
- [ ] `Reopen(closedID)` still lands on `open`; `Reopen(activeID)` is a no-op returning it unchanged.
- [ ] REGRESSION GUARD (already passes today, store.go:537-538 — add/keep a test, no code change expected): `Update(closedID, {Status: closed, Title: ...})` (status no-op) returns `ErrImmutable`.
- [ ] Relevant tests pass.

## Files to Modify
- `sdk/tasks/store.go` (Update reopen branch only)
- `sdk/tasks/close_reopen_test.go` / related `*_test.go`

## Ordering
Edits `store.go`; sequenced AFTER at-dny.6 to avoid concurrent edits to the same file.

title: In-process write serialization (mutex + flock)
description:
## Description
Make the store safe for concurrent use within a single process, not just across
processes. Today writes serialize only via `flock`; the spec now requires an
in-process mutex too, so concurrent goroutines in one embedder (e.g. a GUI) serialize
correctly. Specs: ARCHITECTURE-SPEC §6/§7, SDK-SPEC §1/§7, TASK-STORAGE-SPEC §7.

## Instructions
1. `store.go`: add a `sync.Mutex` (or `sync.RWMutex`) field to `Store`.
2. In `withLock` (the wrapper every mutation already uses), acquire the in-process
   mutex BEFORE the `flock` and release it after, so the order is: in-process lock ->
   flock -> fn -> unflock -> in-process unlock. All existing writers (Create, Update,
   Close, Reopen, AddComment/Edit/Delete, AddDep/RemoveDep) inherit it via withLock.
3. Reads stay lock-free (unchanged) — the contract is "writers serialize against all
   others; reads are lock-free".
4. Keep it minimal: no context plumbing, no API signature changes (the SDK stays
   ctx-free). Just the in-process mutex + flock.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Store` holds an in-process write mutex; `withLock` takes it around every mutation.
- [ ] Concurrent goroutine writes to one `*Store` serialize and all succeed (race test under `-race`).
- [ ] No deadlock: a write that internally calls other write helpers does not re-lock fatally (verify withLock is the single lock point; helpers run inside it).
- [ ] Reads remain lock-free; no API signature changes.
- [ ] `go test -race ./...` for the package passes.

## Files to Modify
- `sdk/tasks/store.go` (Store struct, withLock)
- `sdk/tasks/*_test.go` (concurrent-writers race test)

## Ordering
Edits `store.go`; sequenced AFTER at-dny.2 (which also edits store.go) and BEFORE
at-dny.7 to avoid concurrent edits to the same file.
close_reason: Closed

title: CLI: create --creator + creator in issueDTO
description:
## Description

title: Structured Criteria + Build + Find/FindPage
description:
## Description
Add a typed selection builder that COMPILES to a canonical filter expression fed to
the existing engine — sugar over the one query language, not a second engine. Specs:
SDK-SPEC §3 (Criteria/LabelMatch/WorkState/Build), §4 (Find/FindPage/FindOptions);
QUERY-SPEC §5/§7.

## Instructions
1. New pure file `sdk/tasks/criteria.go` (no vfs/os import — pure-core):
   - `Criteria` (Text, Statuses, Types, Labels, LabelMatch, Assignee, Creator,
     Parent *string, Work, PriorityMin/Max, Created/Updated/Closed From/To).
   - `LabelMatch` (LabelMatchAll = iota default, LabelMatchAny); `WorkState`
     (WorkAny = iota, WorkReady, WorkBlocked).
   - `func (c Criteria) Build() (string, error)`:
     - AND non-empty groups at top level; wrap each multi-value group in parens.
     - ALWAYS quote string/enum/date values (escape `"`->`\"`, `\`->`\\`); emit
       `priority` bare. Empty criteria -> "".
     - Date bounds are HALF-OPEN: `*From` -> `field >= "From"` (inclusive);
       `*To` -> `field < "To"` (exclusive).
     - Priority: emit `priority >= n` / `priority <= n` bare. A positive bound above
       the storable range (e.g. `>= 5`) is emitted AS-IS (harmless — matches all/none
       once the parser accepts it, at-dny.8). A NEGATIVE bound is rejected.
     - Validation: return a `*ValidationError` (NOT a plain error, NOT *ParseError)
       naming the offending field for: unknown `Status`/`Type` (`.Valid()` false) and
       any negative priority bound. For valid input, output always parses.
     - `Parent` non-nil "" -> `parent == ""`.
2. Add `FindOptions{IncludeClosed, Sort, Reverse, Offset, Limit}` (Offset/Limit behave
   as on Filter). Define `Find`/`FindPage` IN `criteria.go` (methods on *Store, to keep
   them out of store.go/ready.go and avoid file conflicts):
   - `Find(c, opt)`  = `List(Filter{Expr: c.Build(), ...})`
   - `FindPage(c, opt)` = `ListPage(Filter{Expr: c.Build(), ...})`
   - On `Build` error, return it and run no scan.
3. Cold scope: do NOT inspect the struct. Derive it from the BUILT expression via the
   same cold-scope predicate `List` uses (QUERY-SPEC §5), so a `Criteria` and its
   hand-written `Expr` scope identically; `FindOptions.IncludeClosed` overrides.
4. Map every field to our actual grammar (status/type/label/assignee/creator/parent ==;
   text/assignee/creator/label ~; priority/dates ordering; ready/blocked bare).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] L1 table tests for `Build()`: each field, combinations, escaping edges (embedded `"`, `\`, spaces), empty `parent`, empty -> "".
- [ ] Date `*From`/`*To` emit `>=` / `<` (half-open) correctly.
- [ ] Unknown Status/Type AND negative priority bound -> `*ValidationError` (field named); positive out-of-range bound emitted as-is, no error.
- [ ] `Build()` output always parses (round-trip; no *ParseError) for valid input.
- [ ] L2: `Find(c)` == structured intent on a storetest fixture (incl. find-by-creator); a Criteria and its hand-written Expr scope identically (cold-scope parity).
- [ ] `LabelMatchAll` is the default.
- [ ] Relevant tests pass.

## Files to Modify
- `sdk/tasks/criteria.go` (new — Criteria/Build/Find/FindPage/FindOptions)
- `sdk/tasks/criteria_test.go` (new), L2 find tests

## Dependencies
Needs at-dny.1 (ListPage/Page/Offset), at-dny.2 (queryable creator), at-dny.8
(parser accepts non-negative priority, so emitted out-of-range bounds parse).
close_reason: Closed

title: creator field in the engine (model, storage, validation, create, query)
description:
## Description
Add `creator` as a first-class, queryable issue field: who filed the issue, set once
at creation, never edited. The SDK applies NO identity default — an empty
`CreateInput.Creator` is stored empty (the `$USER` default is a CLI concern, see

1. `model.go`: add `Creator string` to `Issue` (after `Assignee`).

title: Paging: Filter.Offset + ListPage/Page + cross-partition read dedup
description:
## Description
Add presentation-side paging plus cross-partition read consistency to the engine —
both are `List` refactors, so they share one owner (this task). Specs: SDK-SPEC §3
(Filter), §4 (ListPage/Page), §7 + TASK-STORAGE-SPEC §7 (read dedup).

## Instructions
1. `sdk/tasks/ready.go`: add `Offset int` to `Filter` (applied after sort/reverse,
   before Limit; 0 = none).
2. Refactor `List` so the selection/scope/sort core is shared, then add
   `func (s *Store) ListPage(f Filter) (Page, error)` and
   `type Page struct { Issues []*Issue; Total int }`. `Total` = count of all matches
   in scope BEFORE offset/limit; `Issues` = `matches[Offset:Offset+Limit]`
   (`matches[Offset:]` when `Limit == 0`). Clamp negative `Offset`/`Limit` to 0.
3. Apply offset in both `List` and `ListPage` (offset → limit, after sort/reverse).
4. Cross-partition read dedup: when a scan spans hot + `closed/` (IncludeClosed or
   auto cold-scope), dedup the merged set by ID so a concurrent close/reopen (a
   cross-partition move) can never yield the same ID twice. Hot wins on collision.
   Do this IN `List`, right after `all = append(all, closed...)` (ready.go ~361) and
   the idx rebuild (~365-368). Do NOT modify `index()` / `allClosed()` in store.go —
   the dedup is purely List-side, keeping this task entirely within ready.go.
5. Leave `Query` unchanged (no paging form). Every existing sort already breaks ties
   on `id` (deterministic) — keep that.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Filter.Offset` skips correctly; `Offset >= Total` → empty `Issues`, accurate `Total`.
- [ ] `ListPage` returns `Total` (pre-offset/limit) and the correct window from one snapshot.
- [ ] `Limit == 0` returns all matches from `Offset` onward.
- [ ] Negative `Offset`/`Limit` clamp to 0.
- [ ] A cross-partition scan never returns a duplicate ID (dedup test simulating a both-partitions state).
- [ ] Existing `List` callers behave identically when `Offset == 0`.
- [ ] Relevant tests pass.

## Files to Modify
- `sdk/tasks/ready.go` (Filter, List refactor, ListPage/Page, dedup) — ONLY this file for prod code
- `sdk/tasks/*_test.go` (paging + dedup tests; L2 against storetest fixture)

## Note
Single-snapshot only within one call: paging is NOT isolated across calls (a store
mutated between page fetches can skip/repeat at a window boundary) — this is inherent
and documented in SDK-SPEC §4, not a bug to fix.
close_reason: Closed

title: SDK embedder ergonomics: paging, structured search, creator field
description:
## Description
Implement the spec additions in commits 9f32684 + e563fb6 + a5968af ("paging /
structured Criteria / creator", then two "update spec" refinements). These make the
`sdk/tasks` engine easier and safer to embed (e.g. a GUI/viewer) without changing the
core invariants: one selection engine, one writer, file-based, minimal deps.

The specs (SDK / QUERY / STORAGE / CLI / ARCHITECTURE) are the contract — this epic
brings the code up to them.

## Scope
- Paging: `Filter.Offset` + `ListPage`/`Page` (window + total count) and
  cross-partition read dedup-by-ID (read consistency).
- `creator` field: first-class, queryable, immutable; SDK applies no identity default.
- Structured search: `Criteria`/`Build` (compiles to a filter expression) +
  `Find`/`FindPage`/`FindOptions`; `Build` rejects invalid input as `*ValidationError`.
- Concurrency contract: in-process mutex so writers serialize across goroutines too
  (not just processes).
- Lifecycle: reopen-via-`Update` lands on the requested status (not forced `open`).
- Query engine: priority literals are non-negative (out-of-range matches all/none);
  precise cold-scope predicate (`status == "closed"` positive-only; `status != "closed"`
  does not auto-scan cold).

## Success Criteria
- Code matches SDK-SPEC, QUERY-SPEC, TASK-STORAGE-SPEC, CLI-SPEC, ARCHITECTURE-SPEC.
- Additions are non-breaking (existing callers unaffected) except the two intentional
  behavior changes (reopen-status, cold-scope `!=`), which are spec-driven.
- `mise run quality` and the full test suite (incl. conformance) pass.
- Acceptance review confirms spec↔code parity.

## Non-goals
- No CLI `--offset` (paging stays SDK-facing).
- `creator` immutable (no `update --creator`).
- No second selection engine — `Criteria.Build` only emits expression strings.
close_reason: Closed

title: B4: Durability/fault tests + tighten Mem to match osFS
description:
## Description
`vfs.Mem` is weaker than disk (root cause E): it auto-creates parent dirs, has
fully-atomic appends, and cannot model a crash — so durability is structurally
untestable and disk-only failures pass on Mem (e.g. writing to a subdir without
MkdirAll). L3's "fresh Open() in the same process" never models a crash before
the dir-metadata flush, which is how H2 hid.

## Instructions
1. Tighten `Mem`: require an existing parent dir for WriteAtomic/Append (return
   ErrNotExist otherwise); restrict Rename to files (or document file-only).
2. Add a fault/crash-point seam to exercise the rename + parent-dir-fsync path
   (A4): inject failure between rename and dir-sync; assert recovery semantics.
3. Document in TESTING-STRATEGY which guarantees Mem can and cannot prove.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mem rejects writes to a non-existent parent dir (matches osFS).
- [ ] A test exercises the dir-fsync/rename durability path.
- [ ] Fixing Mem surfaces (then fixes) any latent subdir-write bug.

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/mem.go; sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/*_test.go

title: A9: Conformance & locking polish (M2-M6)
description:
## Description
Batch of verified medium findings:
- M2 non-unix `Lock` (vfs/lock_other.go) is a silent no-op (zero mutual
  exclusion); should fail closed (return an error) on unsupported platforms and
  document the FS.Lock platform contract.
- M3 `validateReplaces` (comments.go:375-385) doesn't enforce the spec's "earlier
  in the stream" rule.
- M4 invalid `--sort` value (cmd/query.go:40 -> ready.go default) silently falls
  back to work-order instead of erroring.
- M5 public `Marshal` (frontmatter.go) doesn't truncate timestamps to whole
  seconds -> can emit out-of-schema timestamps / git churn for SDK callers.
- M6 absent `created`/`updated` makes all comparisons return true
  (eval.go:291-302).

## Instructions
Address each sub-item; update the matching spec only if behavior changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Non-unix build fails closed on Lock (or documents + implements real locking).
- [ ] `validateReplaces` rejects a non-earlier target.
- [ ] Invalid `--sort` returns an error listing valid values.
- [ ] `Marshal` truncates timestamps to whole seconds (UTC).
- [ ] Absent created/updated comparison semantics defined + tested.

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/internal/vfs/lock_other.go, lock_unix.go, fs.go; sdk/tasks/comments.go;
  cmd/query.go; sdk/tasks/frontmatter.go; sdk/tasks/internal/query/eval.go

title: A8: Bound recursive walks (parser/eval depth cap; iterative findCycle)
description:
## Description
The query parser/evaluator (parse.go:140-207, eval.go:97) recurse without a depth
cap, and `findCycle` (ready.go:205-247) is recursive DFS run on every write via
`checkRefs`. Deeply nested input / a very deep dependency chain causes a
`fatal error: stack overflow` (uncatchable; crashes any embedding process via the
SDK). (Finding M1.)

## Instructions
1. Add a depth counter to the parser (and evaluator) and return a `*ParseError`
   ("expression nesting too deep") past a sane bound (e.g. 256).
2. Convert `findCycle` to an explicit-stack iterative DFS (or cap depth and
   return an error).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Deeply nested query returns a clean error, no crash (test).
- [ ] Deep dependency chain (e.g. 1e6) does not crash cycle detection (test).

## Files to Modify
- sdk/tasks/internal/query/parse.go, eval.go; sdk/tasks/ready.go
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Parser nesting capped at 256 (ParseError); findCycle iterative; deep-input tests; green.

title: Filter-expression query language (-q / Store.Query)
description:
## Motivation
Issue selection today is a struct of fields on `Filter`. The contract (QUERY-SPEC)
is a small SQL-flavoured predicate language owned by the engine and forwarded
verbatim by the CLI `-q`. One grammar, one implementation.

## Structure (decided: dedicated sub-package)
`sdk/tasks/internal/query` — a PURE package (no disk, no `tasks` import):
`lex.go`, `parse.go`, `ast.go`, `eval.go`, `errors.go`. It compiles an expression
to a `Predicate` over a `Row` interface and returns `*ParseError`. `tasks` provides
a `Row` adapter over `*Issue` (+ computed ready/blocked) and re-exports
`type ParseError = query.ParseError` (SDK-SPEC §6). The parser/evaluator are thus
unit-tested in isolation against fake rows (L1) — 3.1 and 3.2 need no disk and can
start immediately under TDD.

## Contract
docs/specs/QUERY-SPEC.md + SDK-SPEC §4 (`Query`, `Filter.Expr`, `ParseError`) +
CLI-SPEC §3 (`-q`). Layout: docs/implementation/PACKAGE-OVERVIEW.md.

## Scope

the active set, sorted by ID). Callers needing closed history opt in explicitly —
there is no separate Archive() in the contract; closed is read via:
- `List(Filter{IncludeClosed:true})` / `Query` with a closed-referencing
  expression (QUERY-SPEC.md §5), and `atctl list --all` / `search --all`.
Wire the cold reads so:
- ready/list/Labels(default) -> hot only (cheap).
- export, full-text search, all-time Labels, viewer history -> read closed/ too
  via the IncludeClosed path.
- Document the semantics so no caller silently drops closed issues.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Hot-path APIs are O(open) and never open closed/.
- [ ] `List`/`Query` with IncludeClosed (or a closed-referencing expr) return
      hot+closed.
- [ ] `atctl list --all` and `search --all` include closed issues.

title: Ref resolution + checkRefs fall through to closed/
description:
## Scope
Open issues routinely reference closed ones. With closed moved out of the hot
index (TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §9, §10; SDK-SPEC.md §4 "Get falls through to closed/"):
- `Get(id)` and Detail parent/blocker resolution fall through to `.tasks/closed/`.
- `checkRefs` validates refs that live in closed/ via a cheap `stat` (no parse).
- ready/blocked: a blocker in closed/ counts as resolved; a ref present in
  NEITHER hot nor closed/ is a dangling reference and a validation error — never
  silently treated as satisfied.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Creating/updating an open issue whose parent/blocker is closed validates OK.
- [ ] A dangling ref (neither hot nor closed) still errors.
- [ ] ready() correct when blockers are closed.
close_reason: checkRefs/Get/Detail/ready fall through to closed/ via Stat; closed blocker=resolved; dangling ref=error

title: Close moves file to closed/ and freezes it; Reopen + atctl reopen
description:
## Scope
Implement the lifecycle move and immutability from TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §5 /
SDK-SPEC.md §4 / CLI-SPEC.md §4.
- `Close` stamps closed/close_reason, bumps updated, then atomically MOVES the
  task .md from the hot dir into `.tasks/closed/` (git rename, history preserved).
  The comment sidecar stays in `comments/` — only the task file moves.
- Closed files are immutable in place: reject any in-place write to a file under
  `closed/` (the append-only comment sidecar is the one exception).
- `Reopen` moves the file back to the hot dir, clears closed/close_reason, sets
  status open, bumps updated, re-enables writes. Pre-close status is not persisted.
- CLI: add `atctl reopen <id>`; `atctl update --status closed/<other>` routes
  through Close/Reopen (CLI-SPEC.md §4).

## Acceptance
- [ ] Close relocates the file; a later in-place Update/Close-edit on a closed
      record errors (Reopen excepted).
- [ ] Reopen restores mutability and moves the file back to the hot dir.
- [ ] Appending a comment to a closed issue still works.
- [ ] `atctl reopen` works end to end.
close_reason: Close->closed/ via vfs.Rename (sidecar stays); ErrImmutable in-place; Reopen; atctl reopen; update --status routes Close/Reopen

title: Sidecar primitive: YAML multi-doc stream, append/resolve
description:
## Scope
Implement the comment sidecar as the storage primitive (new `comments.go`),
conforming to TASK-STORAGE-SPEC §4.4. Read/write through the vfs seam; resolve is pure.
Public Store wiring is at-zib.1.2.

## Files to Modify
- NEW `sdk/tasks/comments.go` — sidecar append/read/resolve + helpers.
- NEW `sdk/tasks/comments_test.go`.

## Interface
```go
func (s *Store) commentsPath(id string) string                 // .tasks/comments/<id>.yml
func appendCommentDoc(fs vfs.FS, path string, c Comment) error // fs.Append (O_APPEND+fsync), under the store lock
func readCommentStream(fs vfs.FS, path string) ([]Comment, error) // fs.ReadFile; raw, append order ([] if absent)
func resolveComments(stream []Comment) []Comment               // PURE: collapse replaces-chains; drop tombstones
func sanitizeCommentBody(body string) string                   // PURE: CRLF/CR->LF, strip trailing ws per line
func newCommentID() string                                     // random ^[0-9a-z]{8}$, no stream read
```
- Multi-doc YAML stream, `---` at col 0; append is `fs.Append` under the store lock —
  never rewrite prior docs. `id` self-assigned without reading the stream.
- Sanitize so YAML emits `body: |` (block scalar); a body must not round-trip as a
  double-quoted one-liner. A literal `---` inside a body is safe (indented).
- resolve: each `replaces` chain -> newest doc (later append wins on duplicate
  `replaces`); tombstones omitted.

## Testing
- L1: `resolveComments` (edit/tombstone/chain/duplicate-replaces) + `sanitizeCommentBody`
  + a byte-golden round-trip asserting no `body: "` (no double-quoted scalar) for
  code-fence/trailing-ws/CRLF/`---`-line bodies.
- L2 (Mem): append+read via vfs; guard that writing a sidecar does not change `Store.All()`.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Append+read round-trips arbitrary bodies byte-exact (after sanitization).
- [ ] No reachable input forces double-quoted output.
- [ ] resolveComments handles edit/tombstone/chain/duplicate-replaces.
- [ ] `store.All()` never opens a sidecar.
close_reason: comments.go sidecar primitive: YAML multi-doc, append via vfs, pure resolve/sanitize, rnd-8 id; 27 tests

title: Wire comment SDK API to sidecar; drop comments from frontmatter
description:
## Scope
Wire the sidecar into the public SDK (SDK-SPEC.md §2/§4) and remove comments from
the frontmatter (TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §4.3 "no comments in frontmatter").
- `Comment` struct gains `ID`, `Replaces`, `Deleted` (model.go).
- `AddComment(id,author,body) (*Comment, error)` returns the new comment incl. its
  freshly allocated random id.
- Add `EditComment(id,commentID,author,body) (*Comment, error)` (append revision
  with Replaces; returns new effective comment) and
  `DeleteComment(id,commentID,author) error` (append tombstone).
- Add `Comments(id) ([]Comment, error)`; `Detail.Comments` = resolved effective log.
  All() / ready / list never read the sidecar; Get/Detail/Comments load it lazily.
- Remove `Comments` from the frontmatter struct and `Issue`; migrate existing
  inline frontmatter comments into sidecars on first touch.
- None of add/edit/delete rewrites the task .md (sidecar is append-only).
- Validation (TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §10): reject a body that would serialize as a
  double-quoted scalar; reject a comment with neither body nor `deleted:true`;
  reject `replaces` not naming an existing earlier comment in the same issue.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Frontmatter no longer carries comments; existing stores migrate cleanly.
- [ ] AddComment/EditComment/DeleteComment do not rewrite the issue .md.
- [ ] Comments()/Detail.Comments return the resolved log (edits applied, tombstones
      omitted); on-disk stream keeps full history.
- [ ] Comment validation rejects the §10 cases.
- [ ] All()/ready cost drops measurably (bench in bench/).

title: Cold-partition closed issues into a closed/ subdir (immutable)
description:
## Motivation
Closed issues dominate a mature store (~90%) yet sit in the same flat dir that
`store.All()` scans inside the write lock on every mutation — the dominant O(N)
scaling cost. Move them to a cold `closed/` partition so the hot scan stays
O(open).

## Contract
docs/specs/TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §3 (allocation across all partitions), §5
(lifecycle/partitions/immutability), §9 (ready/blocked across partitions);
docs/specs/SDK-SPEC.md §4 (Get fall-through, Close/Reopen, List+IncludeClosed).

## Measured payoff (dtctl-test corpus, 419 issues; comments already in sidecars)
- Flat dir, hot = 419 files:           All() scan ~7.3ms
- closed/ partition, hot = 41 open:    All() scan ~0.56ms (~13x), and stays flat
  as closed history grows.

`All()` already skips subdirectories, so `closed/` is excluded from the hot scan
with no change to the scan itself — only the move-on-close and the fall-through
reads need building.
close_reason: Closed/cold-partition epic complete: nextID, close-move+reopen, ref fall-through, All() scope; AR closed

title: Comment sidecar: append-only YAML multi-doc with edit/delete
description:
## Motivation
Comments currently live in YAML frontmatter (`Issue.Comments`). Two problems:
1. They bloat the hot scan — comments are ~1/3 of issue-file bytes, parsed on
   every `store.All()` (which runs inside the write lock on every mutation).
2. They belong out of band so the task file stays small and the format can carry
   edit/delete history.

## Contract (replaces the earlier markdown-sidecar sketch)
docs/specs/TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §4.4 + SDK-SPEC.md §2/§4. The sidecar is a
`comments/<id>.yml` **append-only multi-document YAML stream**, one document per
comment in chronological order, separated by `---` at column 0. Per-comment:
`id` (random `^[0-9a-z]{8}$`, self-assigned without reading the stream), `author`,
`created`, optional `replaces` (supersede an earlier comment — edits and deletes),
optional `deleted` (tombstone), and a sanitized block-scalar `body`. Edits/deletes
are append-with-`replaces`; readers resolve each chain to its newest doc
(tombstone -> omitted). Sidecars live in `comments/` for open AND closed issues
and never move.

## Outcome
Comments are gone from the frontmatter; the SDK exposes add/edit/delete/read over
the sidecar returning the resolved effective log; the hot scan never opens a sidecar.
close_reason: Comment sidecar epic complete: primitive + SDK API + CLI; frontmatter clean; AR closed

title: ids: switch to collision-resistant short tokens (beads-style)
description:
## Decision (2026-06-06)
Replace sequential issue IDs (`<prefix>-NNNN`, allocated by max-scan, no counter file) with a **collision-resistant short token modeled on beads**. This removes the parallel-branch merge-collision class by construction (no renumber/doctor needed) and is consistent with the comment-ID scheme, which already uses random 8-char base36 tokens.

## How beads does it (reference)
Repo: github.com/.../beads — `internal/idgen/hash.go`, `internal/utils/issue_id.go`.
- ID = `<prefix>-<token>`, token is **base36** (`0-9a-z`).
- `GenerateHashID` SHA-256s `title|description|creator|timestamp.UnixNano()|nonce`, then base36-encodes the leading bytes. (Content-hash, not pure random.)
- **Adaptive length 3–8 chars** (default 3 = 2 bytes ≈ 16 bits); scale up as the store grows to keep collision probability low.
- **`nonce`**: on collision with an existing ID, bump the nonce and re-hash.
- Prefix extraction treats a 3-char base36 suffix as a hash; 4+ char suffixes require a digit to disambiguate from English words (`isLikelyHash`).

## Residual sub-choice for the implementer
- Pure-random token (simpler; matches our comment IDs) vs beads-style content-hash+nonce (deterministic/reproducible). Pick one.
- Token length + whether to adopt beads' adaptive 3–8 scaling.

## Implementation notes
- Update `sdk/tasks/ids.go` (replace `nextIDFromNames` `%s-%04d`) and callers.
- Keep parsing back-compat for existing on-disk `-NNNN` IDs.
- Update specs: ARCHITECTURE-SPEC ("No counter file") and TASK-STORAGE-SPEC ("Collisions") to describe the new scheme.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New issues get collision-resistant IDs; parallel branches do not collide on merge.
- [ ] Existing `-NNNN` IDs still resolve (back-compat).
- [ ] Specs updated to match.

title: CI: build and test both modules
description:
## Description
GitHub Actions workflow that keeps the spec build green.

title: bench: AddComment timing loop fails on closed issues in synthetic corpus
description:
## Description
Discovered while fixing at-p0j. When bench is RUN (it couldn't compile before), the
AddComment timing loop (bench/main.go ~128-133) calls AddComment on a randomly
chosen issue id; if that id is closed it fatals with "issue is closed and
immutable". Pre-existing, unrelated to the compile fix.

## Repro
`cd bench && go run . -n 10 -mode scaling` (high closed fraction → hits within ~30 tries)

## Fix hint
Filter the ids slice to OPEN issues before the AddComment timing loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bench scaling/redesign modes run to completion with the default corpus.
close_reason: Fixed: bench scaling write-latency loops now filter ids to OPEN issues; scaling/redesign/default modes run to completion.

title: Add per-task event/audit sidecar log (deferred)
description:
Record an append-only audit timeline of task field changes so we can always answer 'when did what happen to this task' and derive label history.

GOAL
- Per-task audit timeline (status/label/priority/dep/parent/assignee/title changes).
- Label history falls out for free from the same diff.

KEY DECISION: audit log, NOT event sourcing.
- The .md files stay the authoritative source of truth (diffable in git, hand-editable).
- The log is best-effort and NOT load-bearing: a missing/corrupt log degrades to 'no history', never to wrong state.
- Rejected full event sourcing: it would demote files to a derived cache, reintroduce the global merge hotspot we avoided by dropping the counter file, and need replay/snapshot machinery for no benefit our use cases require.

STORAGE: per-task sidecar JSONL (e.g. .tasks/history/<id>.jsonl).
- Merge surface aligns with the issue file (conflicts exactly when the issue does); no global append hotspot.
- Rejected single global log (global merge hotspot) and embedded-in-frontmatter (chattier .md diffs) — sidecar keeps .md clean.

SCOPE: task field changes only. NOT comments — comments are already append-only and self-documenting.

MECHANISM
- Add an emit(event) call inside withLock in sdk/tasks/store.go, next to writeIssue.
- 5 of 6 mutators (Create/Close/AddComment-as-noop/AddDep/RemoveDep) map to one event each.
- Update is the interesting one: it already has old (s.Get) and new issue in hand, so diff them and emit granular events (status_changed, label_added/removed, priority_changed, ...). That diff is exactly what yields label history.
- Event shape ~ Comment: {seq, issue, op, field, from, to, actor, at}.

POSSIBLE CLI: atctl log <id> / atctl history (and a global timeline later via scan-and-merge over per-task logs — no authoritative global file needed).

STATUS: deferred by design. Can be added at any time; not urgent. Captured from a design discussion on 2026-06-04.
notes: Format now specified in docs/specs/TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md §4.6 (the source of truth). Note: the spec settled on a multi-document YAML stream (history/<id>.yml), mirroring the comment sidecar §4.4 — NOT the JSONL sketched earlier — for consistency with the rest of the storage format. Spec also adds principle 7 (history is a log, not a ledger of record), and threads the history sidecar through §2 layout, §5 lifecycle (survives close), §7 (best-effort, non-fsync append), and §10 (outside the validation gate). Marked 'specified, not yet implemented' in the spec.
close_reason: Won't do. Decided the per-task history/audit sidecar isn't worth the cost and complexity: git commit history already provides good auditing. Removed from TASK-STORAGE-SPEC.md (reverted principle 7, §2 history/ partition, §4.6, and the §5/§7/§10 references).

title: [decision] active-vs-inactive partition: route deferred/long-parked to cold too
description:
## Question
The hot/cold split is sound, but the real partition may be active-vs-inactive, not strictly open-vs-closed. Long-parked/aspirational/deferred issues inflate the hot set without being actionable (dtctl-test already had 6 deferred).

## Options
- closed/ only (simplest; backlog stays hot).
- closed/ + parked/ (route deferred + long-untouched here; keeps hot = truly active).

## Notes
Hot scan is O(open) at ~13us/file, so even ~2000 hot is ~27ms — degrades gracefully; this is an optimization, not a correctness issue. Decide before compaction/compression design.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Decision recorded in SPEC.md.

title: atctl: agent guide (howto/commands catalog)
description:
## Decision (2026-06-06)
Build the agent-facing command catalog as an `atctl` subcommand that **emits structured YAML** — token-efficient and easy for agents to parse while staying human-readable. The bd-prime / dtctl-commands equivalent.

## Shape (refine in implementation)
- `atctl commands` (and/or `atctl howto`) → YAML on stdout.
- Per command: name, one-line purpose, key flags, a short usage example. Cover every user-facing command: add, close, comment, create, dep, edit, init, labels, list, ready, reopen, rm, search, show, statuses, types, update, version.
- Prefer sourcing from the cobra command tree so the catalog stays in sync automatically rather than a hand-maintained doc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `atctl commands` (or chosen name) prints a concise, valid-YAML catalog of the CLI surface.
- [ ] Every user-facing command is covered with purpose + example.

title: Tombstone comment doc quotes the created timestamp
description:
## Where
sdk/tasks/comments.go marshalCommentDoc() — the tombstone/bodyless `else` branch uses struct encoding (commentOnDisk), which yaml.v3 quotes as a !!timestamp.

## Expected vs actual
- Expected: `created: 2026-06-05T10:34:15Z` (bare scalar, like every other comment doc; TASK-STORAGE-SPEC §6).
- Actual: `created: "2026-06-05T10:34:15Z"` (double-quoted) on tombstones only.

## Impact
Cosmetic — parses and round-trips fine; only diff-noise/inconsistency on tombstone lines.

## Fix
Unify marshalCommentDoc on the yaml.Node tree path (used by the body branch) for tombstones too, so all scalars emit unquoted. Discovered during umbrella acceptance review.
close_reason: marshalCommentDoc unified on yaml.Node tree; tombstone created now bare scalar; regression test added
